preflight_quotes.arc_md: BikeBrowser+ is an educational 2D Phaser game where the player begins as a child repairing and upgrading bikes in the Sonoran Desert, expands through six regionally-grounded Earth biomes — each with a real culture, language, and resource identity — and ultimately engineers life and ecosystems on an alien planet.
preflight_quotes.ecology_substrate_md: EcologyEntity is the carry-forward system that owns living things in the game world — plants, animals, microbes, and (in Act 2+) regional flora and fauna — as scene-independent typed instances with species ids, observation hooks, foraging hooks, and relational tags sourced from data/ecology.js.
preflight_quotes.biology_substrate_md: The biology workbench is one of the most consequential unbuilt carry-forward systems per arc.md §4. It is a single workbench with three modes — Recipe (Stage 1), Parametric (Stage 2), Simulation (Stage 3) — each more powerful and more abstract than the last.
preflight_quotes.knowledge_state_substrate_md: The Knowledge State substrate at src/renderer/game/systems/knowledgeState/ is the carry-forward system that tracks what the player has seen, has interacted with, and understands across every domain in the game — species, recipes, materials, languages, locations, NPCs, scientific concepts, engineering principles, and cultural knowledge.
preflight_quotes.items_1_8_playbook_md: Status (2026-04-27): Phase 2 design complete. The biology substrate design document has landed at src/renderer/game/systems/biology/biology-substrate.md (paired in the same commit with the ecology substrate at src/renderer/game/systems/ecology/ecology-substrate.md).
preflight_quotes.swarm_orchestrator_md: On each turn, you do exactly one of: A) Dispatch the next agent B) Stop at a human gate and wait C) Resolve a blocker D) Report status if the user asks

sections_modified: §4 (added 'Detailed system specification' pointers on Biology Workbench and Ecology Substrate; added new Knowledge State System subsection with pointer)
§6 (open question count: 9 status markers added — 4 in Architectural/High Priority, 5 in Biology Workbench)
§8.5 (added 2026-04-28 status note pointing at knowledge-state-substrate.md §6 bridge contract)
§8 (added 'System integration declarations in shipped substrates' closing subsection citing all three substrates)

open_questions_marked_refined.status: Refined in biology-substrate.md §15.1 (still open; proposed default: quest-gated at Act 2 transition; blocks Stage 2 implementation, not Stage 1).
Refined in biology-substrate.md §9 (still open; three models surfaced — A pure simulator, B soft consequence, C hard in-world propagation; recommended default Model A for Phase 3).
Refined in biology-substrate.md §7 and §15.7 (still open; proposed default: permanent unlock travels implicitly once narratively introduced).

open_questions_marked_refined.question_text: Knowledge State System data model (NEW high-priority entry).
open_questions_marked_refined.status: Refined in knowledge-state-substrate.md §3 and §13 (substrate exists, 10 sub-questions still open; §8.5 gating clause discharges only when implementation ships).

open_questions_marked_refined.question_text: Does biology start in Act 1 with plants/medicine, Act 2 with greenhouse/life support, or both?
open_questions_marked_refined.status: Refined in biology-substrate.md §2 (Stage 1 introduced Act 1 with plants/medicine; Stage 2 unlocks Act 2 with greenhouse/life-support framing).

open_questions_marked_refined.question_text: How much genetic engineering is appropriate for the target age?
open_questions_marked_refined.status: Refined in biology-substrate.md §15.5 (proposed default: 5 visible parameter dimensions at first Stage 2 unlock, 4 advanced through progression; realistic GE simulation remains out-of-scope).

open_questions_marked_refined.question_text: Do engineered organisms have consequences immediately, or only in Act 3?
open_questions_marked_refined.status: Refined in biology-substrate.md §9 (Stage 3-only by design; Phase-3 default Model A is in-silico-only).

open_questions_marked_refined.question_text: Can the player accidentally damage an ecosystem? If yes, is the consequence reversible?
open_questions_marked_refined.status: Refined in biology-substrate.md §9 (Model C 'hard consequence' requires reversibility tooling per arc.md §3 Act 3; still open).

open_questions_marked_refined.question_text: How to teach ethics without becoming preachy?
open_questions_marked_refined.status: Refined in biology-substrate.md §10 ('Ethics is gameplay, not lecture' — Stage 3 simulator demonstrates consequences via graphed time-series; failure-as-curriculum).

notes.v1_4_entry_summary: Three substrate design documents now shipped and referenced (ecology and biology in commit 784e180; knowledge state in commit 9bc7f5f). Section 4 gains a Knowledge State System subsection plus explicit 'Detailed system specification' pointers on the Biology Workbench and Ecology Substrate subsections. Section 6 marks resolved/refined open questions with status lines (3 architectural/high-priority + 1 new Knowledge State entry + 5 Biology Workbench sub-questions). Section 8.5 records that the knowledge-state design doc has landed and references its §6 bridge contract; a closing 'System integration declarations in shipped substrates' subsection in Section 8 records that the three substrates each declare their primitive integration per §8.1. Existing arc.md prose preserved verbatim except for these additive references, status markers, and Document History entry.
